steps.title: Find each object's speed
steps.content: Object M covers $5x$ inches in $y$ seconds, so its speed is $\frac{5x}{y}$ inches per second. Object N is twice as fast: $2 \cdot \frac{5x}{y} = \frac{10x}{y}$ inches per second.

steps.title: Compute time for object N to travel 40x
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $16y$ — this would be the time at speed $\frac{5x}{2y}$, half of M's speed instead of double. Wrong.
  • B) $4y$ — matches $\frac{40x}{10x/y} = 4y$. Correct.
  • C) $30y$ — far too large; it is the target coefficient minus twice the starting-distance coefficient, not the time at object N's speed. Wrong.
  • D) $8y$ — this is the time M would take to travel $40x$ (since M needs $y$ per $5x$, so $8y$ per $40x$), not N. Wrong.
B is correct.
